title: Replays / Records
description: Capture a live you-agent-factory run as a replay artifact, find the saved recording after shutdown, or re-run from saved history without dispatching live workers again.
openingSummary: Replays / Records is the you-agent-factory reference for capturing live runs as replay artifacts and replaying saved history without dispatching live workers again.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Replays / Records is the web reference for you-agent-factory record and replay behavior. Use it when you need to capture a live factory run as a replay-compatible artifact, find the saved recording after shutdown, or re-run from saved history without dispatching live workers again. It covers record mode versus replay mode, the default recording root, run-control flags, incompatible flag pairs, and copyable record and replay commands.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A recording is a replay-compatible artifact that stores observed runtime history from a live factory run. Record mode starts a live run and writes that history to an artifact — either the default generated path or an explicit --record path. Replay mode reads an existing artifact with --replay and reuses the saved history instead of dispatching live workers again. Normal live you run invocations record by default when you pass neither --record nor --replay. This page is the record and replay documentation surface; it is not factory-session pause, resume, or status inspection.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start from a normal live you run when you want a recording written by default. On shutdown, the CLI prints Recording saved: <path> so you can find the artifact after a failure or an unexpected stop. Use the run-controls table below to skip recording, write to an explicit path, or replay a saved artifact. Do not combine the incompatible flag pairs on the same invocation.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web record and replay reference for you-agent-factory. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not factory-session pause or resume, not mock-worker selection contracts, and not a logs, metrics, or API reference.

links.runControlsDefaultEffect: Record to the generated path under ~/.you-agent-factory/recordings/
links.runControlsNoRecordEffect: Skip the default recording for one invocation
links.runControlsRecordEffect: Write the replay artifact to an explicit path you own
links.runControlsReplayEffect: Replay an existing artifact instead of starting a live run
links.incompatiblePairsLabel: Incompatible combinations (rejected for the same invocation)
